classification_hints: question asks for definitions or purpose of accounting
question tests accounting equation
common_misconceptions: book-keeping and accounting are the same

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve ledger accounts, but 1.2 focuses on the equation while 2.1 focuses on double entry processing

scope: purposes of measuring profit and loss
role of accounting in monitoring progress and decision-making
out_of_scope: management accounting for internal decision-making beyond IGCSE scope
classification_hints: question asks why accounting is needed or difference from book-keeping

scope: meaning of assets, liabilities and owner's equity
out_of_scope: complex entity structures not covered in IGCSE
classification_hints: question requires use of Assets = Capital + Liabilities
common_misconceptions: liabilities are subtracted on the wrong side of the equation

common_misclassifications.reason: both use ledger account concepts, but equation questions do not require full double entry recording

classification_hints: question involves recording transactions from documents
common_misconceptions: trade discount and cash discount are recorded the same way

common_misclassifications.reason: both may involve journal entries, but 2.3 records normal transactions while 3.2 corrects errors

topics.topic_name: The double entry system of book-keeping
classification_hints: question asks to prepare or balance ledger accounts
common_misconceptions: debit always means increase

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve recording transactions; 2.1 focuses on ledger accounts, 2.3 on books of prime entry

scope: invoice, debit note, credit note, statement of account, cheque, receipt
out_of_scope: complex commercial terms not in syllabus
classification_hints: question names or requires completion of business documents
common_misconceptions: debit note and credit note have the same effect

scope: cash book, petty cash book, sales/purchases/returns journals, general journal
classification_hints: question asks which book of prime entry to use or requires posting from books of prime entry
common_misconceptions: cash discount is recorded in the sales journal

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve recording; 2.3 focuses on prime entry books before ledger

out_of_scope: control accounts as part of double entry system
reconciling control account balances with sales/purchases ledger balances
classification_hints: question involves checking or correcting records
common_misconceptions: all errors affect the trial balance

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve ledger entries; verification focuses on checking correctness

scope: statement of ledger balances on a particular date
out_of_scope: errors affecting trial balance are covered in 3.2
classification_hints: question asks to prepare or amend a trial balance
common_misconceptions: a trial balance proves there are no errors

common_misclassifications.reason: both deal with errors; 3.1 identifies errors not affecting trial balance, 3.2 corrects them

scope: use suspense account as temporary measure
adjust profit/loss and statement of financial position
out_of_scope: errors not in the syllabus list
classification_hints: question asks to correct errors using journals or suspense account
common_misconceptions: suspense account is a permanent account

common_misclassifications.reason: both deal with errors; 3.2 focuses on correction

scope: use and purpose of bank statement
out_of_scope: complex bank adjustments not listed in syllabus
classification_hints: question provides bank statement and cash book requiring reconciliation
common_misconceptions: unpresented cheques are added to the bank statement balance only

common_misclassifications.reason: both adjust the cash book; bank reconciliation is a specific verification technique

scope: purposes of purchases/sales ledger control accounts
out_of_scope: control accounts as part of double entry system
reconciliation of control account balances with ledger balances
classification_hints: question asks to prepare sales/purchases ledger control account
common_misconceptions: control accounts are part of the double entry system

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve ledger accounts; control accounts are memorandum accounts for verification

scope: capital vs revenue expenditure and receipts
classification_hints: question involves year-end adjustments or asset accounting
common_misconceptions: capital expenditure is recorded as an expense immediately

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve financial statements; 4 covers adjustments, 5 covers preparation

topics.topic_name: Capital and revenue expenditure and receipts
scope: distinguish and account for capital/revenue expenditure and receipts
effect on profit and asset valuations
classification_hints: question asks to classify expenditure/receipts
common_misconceptions: revenue expenditure increases asset value

topics.topic_name: Accounting for depreciation and disposal of non-current assets
scope: ledger accounts and journal entries for depreciation
sale of non-current assets including disposal accounts
classification_hints: question involves depreciation calculation or disposal of asset
common_misconceptions: depreciation is a cash expense

common_misclassifications.reason: both may involve financial statements; 4.2 focuses on non-current asset adjustment

classification_hints: question requires adjusting for accruals or prepayments
common_misconceptions: accrued expenses are assets

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve year-end adjustments; 4.3 is the adjustment technique

topics.topic_name: Irrecoverable debts and provision for doubtful debts
scope: meaning of irrecoverable debts and recovery
out_of_scope: detailed IFRS 9 expected credit loss model
classification_hints: question involves bad debts or provision for doubtful debts
common_misconceptions: provision for doubtful debts is an expense in the same way as irrecoverable debts

scope: lower of cost and net realisable value
effect on gross profit, profit, equity and asset valuation
out_of_scope: FIFO/LIFO/AVCO inventory costing methods not required at IGCSE
classification_hints: question asks to value inventory or explain effect of valuation
common_misconceptions: inventory should always be valued at selling price

classification_hints: question asks to prepare a full set of financial statements
common_misconceptions: all businesses use the same financial statement format

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve final accounts; 5 focuses on statement preparation

scope: income statements and statements of financial position
classification_hints: question concerns a single owner business
common_misconceptions: drawings are an expense

scope: income statements/appropriation accounts/statements of financial position
interest on loans/capital/drawings, salaries, profit share
classification_hints: question involves two or more partners sharing profits
common_misconceptions: partners' salaries are expenses in the income statement

common_misclassifications.reason: both prepare financial statements; partnership has appropriation account

scope: income statements, statements of changes in equity, statements of financial position
classification_hints: question involves limited company financial statements
common_misconceptions: debentures are part of equity

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve business structures; limited company uses share capital and debentures

scope: receipts and payments accounts vs income and expenditure accounts
classification_hints: question concerns a not-for-profit organisation
common_misconceptions: accumulated fund is the same as capital

common_misclassifications.reason: both prepare financial statements; clubs use receipts/payments and accumulated fund

scope: direct material, direct labour, prime cost, factory overheads
manufacturing accounts, income statements, statements of financial position
classification_hints: question involves a manufacturing business and cost of production
common_misconceptions: factory overheads are part of prime cost

common_misclassifications.reason: both prepare financial statements; manufacturing includes cost of production

scope: opening and closing statements of affairs
calculate profit/loss from changes in capital
calculate sales/purchases/gross profit/trade receivables/trade payables from incomplete info
prepare financial statements from incomplete records
out_of_scope: incomplete records for partnerships or companies
classification_hints: question states some records are missing and asks to calculate missing figures
common_misconceptions: mark-up and margin are the same percentage

common_misclassifications.reason: both prepare financial statements; incomplete records require reverse calculations

classification_hints: question asks to calculate or interpret ratios
common_misconceptions: a high current ratio is always good

common_misclassifications.reason: both use financial statement data; 6 focuses on analysis rather than preparation

topics.topic_name: Calculation and understanding of accounting ratios
classification_hints: question asks to calculate a ratio
common_misconceptions: ROCE uses profit after interest

scope: recommendations for improving profitability and working capital
classification_hints: question asks to comment on ratios
common_misconceptions: higher profit margin always means better management

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve ratios; 6.1 is calculation, 6.2 is interpretation

scope: apply accounting ratios to inter-firm comparison
classification_hints: question compares two different businesses
common_misconceptions: ratios can be directly compared without considering business differences

scope: owners, managers, trade payables, banks, investors, club members, governments/tax authorities
classification_hints: question asks who uses accounting information and why
common_misconceptions: all users want the same information

classification_hints: question asks why accounting information has limitations
common_misconceptions: financial statements show the current value of the business

classification_hints: question asks about accounting concepts or policies
common_misconceptions: prudence means always underestimating profit

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve concepts; 7 focuses on principles and policies

classification_hints: question names or applies a specific accounting principle
common_misconceptions: consistency means never changing a policy

scope: objectives in selecting policies: comparability, relevance, reliability, understandability
classification_hints: question asks why a particular accounting policy is chosen
common_misconceptions: accounting policies can be changed freely to manipulate profit

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve concepts; 7.2 focuses on policy selection
